mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in more than 50% of rolling 2-year windows, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.0%, further anticipated tightening compresses equity valuations via higher discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 1.0% over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) signal a demand-pull expansion that outpaces slack absorption, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint occurrence of RSAFS increase and UNRATE decrease fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months over any 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation regime, an increase in the federal funds rate signals policy normalization that confirms output gap closure and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days; this direction reverses in high-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: If in low-inflation regimes positive FEDFUNDS changes are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) relative to its 12-month trend signals manufacturing sector momentum that translates into positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month INDPRO increases are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,200 thousand units, the resulting contraction in residential investment drags on cyclical earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST < 1200 is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of accelerating payroll growth (PAYEMS) and declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals a profit-squeezing cost shock in which rising labor inputs occur without output gains, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the simultaneous occurrence of PAYEMS increase and INDPRO decrease is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) month-over-month signals robust consumer demand that boosts near-term earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS monthly increases are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations over any 1-year rolling window, reject.
